query_normalized: the impact of network centrality on neural synchrony in resting-state fmri neuroscience
rationale: This paper satisfies acceptance criteria (a) and (b) because it directly measures network centrality in resting-state fMRI (the user's independent variable) and examines its relationship to neural synchrony/connectivity metrics (transfer entropy, mutual information, Pearson correlation, wavelet correlation - the user's dependent variable) within the same rs-fMRI domain.
